scenario: Yarrow Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 with 16 Spring Boot services activating 14000 jobs per hour and tunes camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval to PT0.05S. The platform team must migrate the YAML key to the documented 8.8 replacement before the next config audit.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval with camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval at the unified worker level.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval with camunda.client.worker.override.poll-interval at the unified worker level.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval with camunda.client.poll-interval at the unified worker level.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval with camunda.client.zeebe.poll-interval at the unified worker level.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe.defaults section maps poll-interval to camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval as the documented 8.8 replacement.

b.text: Incorrect. Override entries apply per job type; the defaults poll-interval migrates to camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval instead.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no flat camunda.client.poll-interval entry in the 8.8 reference; poll-interval lives under worker.defaults.

d.text: Incorrect. The zeebe segment is dropped in the unified prefix; the documented replacement uses the worker.defaults path instead.

explanation: camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.poll-interval migrates to camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval in 8.8. The property controls the maximal interval between polls for new jobs.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
